narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads D1 CSM at 100 in a Risk-Off High regime, but the critical override is USD H4 CSM fading hard to 44 versus D1's 100, a 56-point divergence that is the single most important signal in this brief, meaning intraday USD longs carry unwinding risk despite hawkish Fed narrative. USDCAD D1+H4 Strong Buy with CADJPY H4 Strong Sell at -4 and CAD sitting weakest on D1 CSM at 18 makes USDCAD the cleanest expression; GBPJPY D1+H4 Strong Buy at +6 with GBPUSD correlation at +0.87 amplifies the JPY-weakness trade across the GBP cluster.
AUD H4 CSM accelerating to 70 versus D1 at 31 flags AUDUSD and AUDJPY as short-term counter-trend setups against USD.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPJPY CMP 54 with H4 67 accelerating, AUDJPY CMP 59 with both TFs aligned upward, NZDJPY CMP 58 H4 rising
Fading: EURUSD CMP 41 D1 at 32 fading, GBPUSD CMP 43 D1 at 29 fading

WATCH
Iran deal confirmation hits wires, USDCAD sells off sharply as risk-on flow hits CAD longs directly, invalidating D1 Strong Buy setup
Fed speaker reaffirms hawkish rate path, USDJPY extends D1 Buy with H4 momentum at 54 needing reclaim of 60 to confirm continuation
Copper sustains above 6.40, AUDUSD H4 acceleration confirmed and AUDJPY targets next resistance with NZDUSD correlation cluster at +0.90 joining the move

themes.theme: US Dollar strength from hawkish Fed messaging
Iran nuclear deal negotiations creating risk-on sentiment
Japanese Yen weakness from energy prices and USD strength
Euro under pressure from weak PMIs and geopolitical uncertainty
Canadian Dollar weakness amid US-Iran uncertainty and Fed hawkishness
Risk-on flows from potential Iran peace deal resolution

key_observation: USD bid supported by hawkish Fed rhetoric from Waller and PCE data, offset partially by risk-on flows from Iran deal optimism. Equity markets climbing on peace deal hopes and chipmaker strength. Conflicting signals between safe-haven demand and risk appetite creating mixed directional flow.
watch: Iran negotiations critical stage - outcome could trigger major risk-on reversal. Fed speakers and any additional hawkish commentary. US consumer sentiment data weakness (UMich miss). Japanese intervention data and Tokyo CPI. Eurozone PMI deterioration.
